build_status.note: Full A-to-Z build-out complete: every phase (0 through 5) carries tasks and milestones are finalized. Phase-5 remains deferred:true (recorded-now / built-later, cloud gated on traction per PRD §14). All stories are derived from PRD-rowshape-v1.md and RFC-0001.

project.name: rowshape
project.tagline: The type-checker for database migrations — a human and an agent get the same answer through the same contract.
project.one_liner: Execute a proposed schema change against production-shaped data in a disposable environment and return a machine-readable verdict.

license.rationale: PRD §8.4 — the moat is being the default in the agent loop; every restriction taxes exactly that. MIT chosen, not defaulted.

repos.name: rowshape/rowshape
repos.purpose: CLI + MCP server (single Go binary), OSS core

repos.name: rowshape/fixture-spec
repos.purpose: The Rowshape Fixture Spec (RFC-0001), own repo, own version

repos.name: rowshape/corpus
repos.purpose: (migration, fixture, expected verdict) triples — the regression suite and credibility asset

cli.rationale: PRD §7 — single static binary no runtime; native dialect of the category; hydrate is CPU-bound; advantages compound over time.
explicitly_no: gofakeit / general fake-data library (own synthesis engine — PRD §16)
distribution: FROM scratch Docker image for CI

marketing_and_docs.rationale: PRD §9 — fits existing Pages standard, purpose-built for docs+landing, ships zero JS by default. The Go decision was about the binary, not the website.

api.rationale: PRD §9 — RFC §11 canonical form must have exactly ONE implementation. If the server recomputes canonicalization in TypeScript, digests eventually disagree and attestations silently stop verifying. Not Deno edge functions for this reason.
api.phase: 5 (recorded now, built later; constrains v1 verdict shape via §9.1)

datastore.service: self-hosted Supabase (on Coolify, alongside the API)
datastore.rationale: PRD §9 — single VPS is defensible because validate never calls the cloud, fixtures are committed to git (registry is convenience not dependency), and fixtures are value-free by design so a breach exposes statistics not customer data.

loop.description: This file is the single source of truth for the build loop (Claude /loop). The work signal is `passes`: every story with `passes: false` is unfinished and is work to do. Each iteration: read this file, select the next actionable `passes: false` story, do exactly that story, verify it, flip `passes` to true only when it truly passes, commit. Repeat until no `passes: false` stories remain.
loop.work_signal: passes (boolean). false = needs work, true = done and verified. To list all remaining work: every story where passes == false.
operating_rules: Study the referenced spec section(s) in `spec_refs` BEFORE implementing a task. The spec wins over intuition.
Do exactly ONE story per iteration. Do not batch or skip ahead.
A story may only be selected when every id in its `depends_on` has `passes: true`.
Never set `passes` to true without every `acceptance_criteria` met AND every `verification` command passing.
Never weaken an invariant, downgrade a confidence level, or relax an acceptance criterion to make a task pass. If blocked, set status `blocked` and record why.
Respect all entries in `invariants` on every task, in every phase, forever.
Prefer the smallest change that satisfies the task. No speculative surface area.
After each task: commit with the task id in the message, then re-read this file before selecting the next.
Check `kill_criteria` at the named checkpoints; if a threshold is hit, stop and surface it rather than pushing on.
loop.task_selection: Lowest phase order first; within a phase, lowest `priority` number among stories with `passes: false` whose `depends_on` are all `passes: true`.
loop.status_note: `status` is optional human context (use `blocked` to record a stuck story). `passes` is the authoritative done-signal the loop reads.
stop_conditions: No `passes: false` stories remain in any non-deferred phase.
A milestone marked as the launch gate (see `milestones`) is satisfied.
A kill_criteria threshold is triggered (stop and surface, do not auto-continue).

invariants.statement: The verdict is one Go `Verdict` struct with two marshalers (JSON + human). Human output is always a rendering of the JSON, never a separate code path. CLI, MCP server, and GitHub Action all render the same struct.

The verdict contract (fields, codes, exit codes) is the public API. Finding codes are permanent and namespaced (RS-LOCK, RS-DATA, RS-CONSTRAINT, RS-INDEX, RS-PERF, RS-REVERSE). Exit codes: 0 PASS, 1 FAIL, 2 WARN-only, 3 tool error. `remediation` is mandatory on every error.

A verdict is capped by the minimum confidence across the facts it relied on. exact/measured -> PASS allowed; estimated/declared/absent -> WARN max. A finding resting on estimated facts CANNOT produce PASS; it produces WARN naming the command that resolves it. Findings MUST NOT downgrade a dependency's confidence to reach a stronger verdict.
invariants.priority: This is the one thing that must never be wrong (a wrong PASS is unrecoverable).

invariants.statement: `unique` MUST be `exact` or absent. Uniqueness is NEVER inferred from a sample.

Durations are reported as buckets (instant/fast/noticeable/slow/outage) with the extrapolation basis attached, never as point estimates. Extrapolation is model-based (per operation class) and engine-version-conditional; refuse to extrapolate when engine.version is absent.

A fixture contains no rows — only statistics. Never emit `range`/min/max on text or bytea columns. `strict` privacy must be supported; emitters MUST NOT default to `permissive`. `inspect --leaks` must enumerate every value-derived field.

Canonical form (keys sorted lexicographically, two-space indent, no anchors/aliases, floats to 6 sig figs, \n line endings, meta.digest + meta.generated_at excluded) has exactly ONE implementation, in Go, shared by CLI and API.

Verdicts are emitted in a DSSE/in-toto-signable predicate shape from day one (subject = fixture digest + migration file digests; predicateType = https://rowshape.com/attestation/v1; predicate = verdict body incl depends_on + confidence). No signing infra in v1 — just a format that won't need retrofitting.
`validate` is free forever and never calls the cloud. The moment `validate` is gated, the agent loop breaks and the wedge dies.

No `rowshape apply` in v1. `validate` never touches a non-disposable DB (hard-refuse if target host matches the fixture source host). `verify` is read-only. `pull` requires a read-only role; refuse superuser without --i-know.

hydrate --seed N produces identical output for the same fixture + hydrator version across platforms. Per-column PRNG derived via hash(seed, table, column, row_ordinal); generation order must not depend on Go map iteration order (canonicalize first).
Ship SBOM and cosign signatures from the first release. Single static binary, no runtime, deps kept deliberately few.

kill_criteria.test: If pull + hydrate don't produce a database that reproduces a known corpus pathology, the fixture premise is wrong. Stop before building validate on top of it.
If the agent-loop demo doesn't work end-to-end without a human turn, the wedge isn't real. Reassess whether a 3rd linter in this market is worth continuing.
If there are zero unsolicited issues or PRs from people you didn't tell about it, that's information — do not push harder on momentum alone.

phases.name: Foundation — claim, spec, skeleton
phases.ships: Claim rowshape.com, GitHub org, npm name, Go module path. Fixture Spec RFC public. Repo + goreleaser skeleton.
definition_of_done: All namespaces reserved and the Go module path is fixed and documented.
rowshape/fixture-spec is a public repo containing RFC-0001 versioned `1`, MIT.
rowshape/rowshape is a Go module that builds a single binary with stub subcommands and MIT license.
goreleaser produces signed, SBOM-accompanied artifacts on all five platforms across every distribution channel.
CI builds, tests, and lints on every push.

Build + test + lint jobs are green on the skeleton.
Workflow is matrix-ready for the PG 11–17 version matrix added in phase 2.
verification: CI green on a trivial PR
